title: Roasted Crisp Venezuelan Challots Gratin
description: A comforting Venezuelan dish featuring roasted challots in a crispy earthenware-cook, perfect for a low-carb-friendly meal. This recipe is a twist on traditional Venezuelan cuisine, with a focus on bold flavors and textures.
instructions: Peel and chop the challots into 1-inch pieces.
Heat the ghee in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the olive oil, cumin, chili powder, salt, and pepper to the skillet. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the challot pieces to the skillet and cook for 5 minutes, until they start to brown.
Transfer the challot mixture to a ttukbaegi earthenware-cook or a small cast-iron dish.
Top the challot mixture with the shredded parmesan cheese and chopped cilantro.
Bake the dish in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, until the top is golden brown and the challots are tender.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the Roasted Crisp Venezuelan Challots Gratin hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Garnishes may be omitted for a more rustic presentation.
